medications.indication_rationale: Apply from the jawline/neck down to the soles (webs, navel, genitals), leave 8-14 h, wash off; REPEAT in 7 days to kill newly hatched mites.
medications.dose_route_frequency: whole-body application overnight, repeat in 7 days (as per source note)

medications.indication_rationale: Night-time itch/sleep relief; the post-scabetic itch persists after mites are killed.
medications.dose_route_frequency: e.g., 25 mg at night (as per source note)

medications.drug_name: repeated nightly permethrin for the persistent itch
medications.indication_rationale: Post-scabetic itch is immune, not live mites — repeated application irritates/burns the skin; use antihistamines and emollients instead.

behavioural_change_actions.specific_action: Treat every household/close contact simultaneously tonight (even if not itching), hot-wash all bedding/towels/recently worn clothes (or bag for 72h), and repeat the cream in exactly 7 days.
behavioural_change_actions.rationale: Prevents reinfestation and eradicates the infestation.

patient_education_points.point: This is scabies — a mite in the top layer of skin. The cream kills the mites tonight, but everyone in the house must be treated at the same time, even if they're not itching yet, or it will bounce back.

The itch will keep going for 2–4 weeks even after the mites are dead (it's an allergic reaction) — use antihistamines and moisturiser, and DON'T re-apply the cream every night. Repeat the cream once at day 7.
If the scratched areas become hot, red, swollen or ooze pus, or you develop a fever — seek medical care.

follow_up_plan.what_to_reassess: Eradication, post-scabetic itch/nodules, and any secondary infection.
follow_up_plan.trigger_for_earlier_review: Secondary infection (hot/red/pus/fever), or widespread crusting — seek review.

must_include_items: Diagnose scabies (triad) and prescribe permethrin neck-to-toe with a repeat at 7 days
Simultaneous treatment of ALL household/close contacts + hot-washing/decontamination
Counsel that post-scabetic itch persists 2-4 weeks (antihistamine; don't over-apply permethrin)
acceptable_alternatives: Oral ivermectin for treatment failure/crusted scabies or where topical is impractical
errors_of_commission: Advising nightly permethrin for the persistent itch
scoring_rubric.passing_threshold_notes: Pass requires permethrin + repeat at 7 days + simultaneous contact treatment + post-scabetic-itch counselling, with a secondary-infection safety-net.
